title: Wars will be more integrated, theatre-oriented in future: General Dwivedi
content: General Upendra Dwivedi, an accomplished military leader with over 40 years of service who retires on June 30, was accorded a guard of honour as part of a ceremonial farewell held on the lawns of the South Block

title: SC orders status quo on Karnataka HC direction to reopen ethanol allocation process
content: Attorney General R. Venkataramani submitted that the 20% ethanol blending programme in petrol is still an ongoing experiment and the impact of the policy would become clearer by next year

title: Stalin sets up panel to recommend revamp of DMK organisation
content: Mr. Stalin said he had earlier constituted 19 teams, each comprising two members, to visit all Assembly constituencies in the State and gather the views of party cadres and functionaries

title: SIR in Karnataka: CM says citizens without voting rights may risk losing government benefits
content: The Chief Minister says the SIR process had formally begun in the State and urged voters to ensure that their details in the electoral rolls were up to date

title: Shivraj Chouhan assures seamless shift to VB-G RAM G; Kharge raises questions on MGNREGA dues
content: The Rural Development Minister says no eligible worker will be left without work; the Congress chief cites pending payments to States under MGNREGA and funding concerns under new employment law

title: Budget tax proposal lifts spirit of long-pending feni project in Kannur
content: The ‘Kannur Feni’ project of Payyavoor Service Cooperative Bank is awaiting final approval from the Excise Minister after nearly a decade of regulatory and administrative processes

title: University students’ union polls in Calicut varsity on August 7
content: Election Grievance Cell finds irregularities in the conduct of the college union elections in the previous academic year

title: Woman and newborn found dead in Thrissur lodge
content: Police question autorickshaw driver who accompanied 30-year-old at check-in; probe under way into circumstances surrounding deaths

title: Row over proposal to appoint Seshadrinathan as State Election Commissioner intensifies
content: Kerala Pradesh Congress Committee general secretary P.M. Niyas petitions Home Minister, urging him to order an antecedents verification of Seshadrinathan, examining his political positions and relations in the past 15 years. He also requests Chennithala to share the intelligence report on the judge with the Local Self-Governments Minister

title: NCLAT upholds PF, gratuity dues for Jet Airways employees, rejects SBI appeal
content: Ruling ensures former employees need not compete with banks and other creditors for their PF and gratuity dues

title: Left parties to skip Vijay’s invitation for meeting of post poll allies
content: The leaders of the two Left parties, however, would meet the Chief Minister at Secretariat and bring to his attention many pressing issues of the workers and other sections of the society.

title: Ram Temple donation theft tests VHP’s demand to free temples from state control
content: Describing government control of temples as a ‘threat to Hindu society’, the VHP had launched a nationwide campaign in 2021 to free Hindu temples and religious institutions from government control

title: VHP criticises Faizabad Bar Association’s resolution, says it violates constitutional principles and legal ethics
content: The Supreme Court had held on various occasions that such resolutions were ‘wholly illegal, against all traditions of the Bar, and against professional ethics’, observing that every person has the right to legal representation, says VHP president Alok Kumar, who is also a lawyer

title: India’s tourism sector remains constrained by regulatory complexity, says report
content: Scaling visa facilitation in line with global benchmarks critical to enhancing India’s international accessibility and competitiveness, notes the report released by Union Tourism Minister; Tourism Ministry signs MoU with Google to strengthen digital promotion of landmarks

title: Watch: Why Sachin Ahir’s switch to Shinde faction is a setback for Thackeray| Above the Fold| 30.06.2025
content: The episode takes a look latest political developments in Maharashtra as another Sachin Ahir joins Eknath Shinde’s camp; Haldia Petrochemicals in West Bengal; the rollout of the SIR of electoral rolls across four States and Delhi; rising political tensions over the Ram Temple donation case, and more.

title: U.P. Congress chief, MPs stopped from visiting Ram Temple in Ayodhya, put under house arrest
content: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai says he was arrested from his hotel and is being detained in a State-run university guest house; Amethi MP asks why the right to darshan is being denied on political grounds

title: A.P. Health Minister proposes model to assess performance of govt. hospitals
content: The new model will take into account services to be completed by every doctor, nurse and technician, on a day-to-day basis, says Satya Kumar Yadav

title: Chhattisgarh Deputy CM Vijay Sharma offers prayers at Kanaka Durga temple
content: Accompanied by his family members, Vijay Sharma also participates in special rituals at Indrakeeladri

title: General Dhiraj Seth takes over as 31st Chief of the Army Staff
content: General Seth’s command assignments include an armoured regiment in the desert sector, an armoured brigade in the Western Theatre, a counter-insurgency force in J&K, and command of the Sudarshan Chakra Corps, one of the Army’s premier strike formations

title: Andhra Pradesh aims to emerge as national model in digital connectivity, says Naidu
content: Chief Minister asks telecom operators to strengthen last-mile connectivity and improve mobile coverage within 100 days

title: Aaditya Thackeray’s close confidante Sachin Ahir switches allegiance to Shinde faction, files nomination for Deputy Chairperson election
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Ambadas Danve confirmed that MLC Jagannath Abhyankar is the Maha Vikas Aghadi nominee

title: Markapuram plans 63,000 hectare horticulture expansion over three years
content: Collector says that State government has plans to cultivate orchards in 16,000 hectares in the district during the first phase

title: Bodies of teenaged inter-caste lovers found in Mayiladuthurai district; violence erupts
content: Police launch probe amid suspicion of foul play and caste killing; pickets posted at the village and bodies shifted to Tiruvarur Government Medical College Hospital for autopsy

title: YSRCP leader alleges large-scale irregularities in A.P. Telugu Academy
content: PDF files of Intermediate second-year syllabus were sold to private colleges for ₹10 crore in violation of academy rules, alleges Telugu Academy former Chairperson Nandamuri Lakshmi Parvathi

title: 'Stripped him, forced him to ... ': Temple priest 'abducted, assaulted' in Bangladesh
content: A Hindu law student and temple priest, Subhash Deuri, was allegedly abducted and assaulted in Dhaka, Bangladesh. Unidentified men held him captive, subjected him to torture, and forced him to arrange ransom payments. He was later found unconscious and hospitalized. Authorities are investigating the motive behind the incident, with no police complaint yet lodged.

title: England's Playing XI vs India in 1st T20I: Archer OUT; Buttler and Salt to open
content: England has unveiled their formidable playing XI for the opening T20I against India, captained by Harry Brook.  Boasting over 650 T20 international caps, the seasoned squad features a strong batting lineup including Phil Salt, Jos Buttler, and Brook himself.  With a balanced attack led by Saqib Mahmood and Adil Rashid, England aims to leverage their home advantage in the highly anticipated five-match series opener.

title: Remember endless tabs & rabbit holes? How AI is changing the web we grew up with
content: The internet's evolution from a browsing landscape to an AI-driven answer engine is reshaping user habits. Once a journey of exploration, the web now offers instant, customized responses, saving time but potentially narrowing discovery. This shift impacts digital publishing and marketing, forcing a redefinition of online visibility beyond traditional clicks. While offering convenience, AI browsing also raises significant privacy and security concerns.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has exited New York City real estate at a steep loss, selling his stake in struggling landlord A&E Real Estate for just six cents on the dollar. The Bloomberg-reported sale lands as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze on one million rent-stabilized apartments squeezes landlords already battered by 2019 rent laws, soaring costs and mounting unpaid rent.

title: GatiShakti, reforms & Aatmanirbharta: Inside PM Modi's marathon meet with top govt officials
content: Prime Minister Modi urged government secretaries to enhance 'ease of doing business' and 'ease of living' by leveraging the PM GatiShakti platform for integrated planning and inter-departmental coordination. Discussions focused on deregulation and promoting self-reliance, with officials presenting progress on reforms and citizen-centric initiatives.

title: 20% ethanol blending still an experiment, results by next year: Centre tells SC
content: Appearing for the Centre, Attorney General R Venkataramani argued that any judicial intervention in the allocation process at this stage could unsettle the government's national ethanol blending policy. He submitted that the 20 per cent ethanol blending programme remained an ongoing experiment and that its impact would become clearer by next year, reported news agency PTI.

title: '994 days of genocide': Microsoft engineer quits, emails thousands on his final day
content: A Microsoft data center technician in Italy resigned on his final day, emailing thousands of colleagues across Europe to accuse the company of powering Israel's war in Gaza through its Azure cloud and European data centers. Published by No Azure for Apartheid, the email cites the Unit 8200 surveillance exposé and urges Microsoft workers to "reclaim our labor" and divest.

title: 'Nothing to do with India': England cricketer clarifies after accusing ICC of bias
content: England cricketers Kate Cross and Alex Hartley have faced severe backlash, including death threats, after their criticism of the ICC's Women's T20 World Cup semi-final scheduling was misinterpreted. They clarified their comments targeted the tournament's policy, not the Indian team, which was automatically placed in an earlier slot if they qualified. The controversy became moot when India was eliminated, reverting the schedule to the standard format.

title: Gold gains strategic importance as central banks hedge against geopolitical risks
content: Global central banks are boosting gold reserves amidst rising geopolitical risks, with a significant portion planning to reduce US dollar exposure. A new survey reveals a strategic shift towards gold for protection, while the euro and renminbi are eyed as alternatives, though not without their own challenges. Central banks are also increasingly embracing AI for operational improvements, adapting to a world of persistent uncertainty.

title: ‘Is she having an affair?’ Ketan's father claims in FIR his son suspected Siya's link with Chetan
content: A Pune realtor's tragic fall from Lohagad Fort has led to the arrest of his fiancée and her alleged paramour, believed to be involved in his death. Reports indicate that the victim had voiced concerns regarding infidelity and was troubled by his fiancée's actions before the fatal incident. Ignoring family warnings about her history due to their connections, the investigation now delves deeper into this suspected murder case.

title: Canada's 'Palantir' creates new funding record; CEO says ambition is to change the country
content: Canadian defence tech firm Dominion Dynamics secured a record C$139 million Series A funding, aiming to revitalise the nation's defence capabilities. CEO Eliot Pence envisions building a major Canadian defence prime, focusing on Arctic-hardened surveillance and autonomous platforms. This investment fuels development of their AuraNet software and Scout autonomous system, bolstering Canada's defence industrial strategy amidst rising global military spending.

title: 'Huge distraction': Strauss questions timing of Ben Stokes' retirement
content: Former England captain Andrew Strauss has voiced concerns over Ben Stokes' retirement announcement during the crucial Test against New Zealand, deeming it a significant distraction. Strauss believes such news should be shared either before or after a match to maintain team focus. Stokes, an exceptional all-rounder, retires with numerous records, including the most Test sixes and a rare double of over 7,000 runs and 250 wickets.

title: US stocks today: Wall Street edges higher on quarter-end optimism as Nvidia rebounds
content: US markets saw a mixed trading session as investors weighed a rebound in AI stocks against persistent concerns about interest rates and global tensions. While major indices showed modest gains, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq are set for their first monthly dip in three months. Stronger-than-expected jobs data hints at a resilient economy, but also fuels inflation worries and potential Fed rate hikes.

title: Brittany Mahomes shares heartfelt promise inspired by Patrick Mahomes
content: Brittany Mahomes says becoming a mother has made her more ambitious and intentional about pursuing her goals. She hopes her children, Sterling, Bronze, and Golden, see both her and Patrick Mahomes working hard and following their dreams. Brittany believes leading by example will teach them that success comes through determination and perseverance. She also shared that her daughter Sterling has shown growing interest in her work, making those moments even more meaningful.

title: Maple Leafs linked to blockbuster trade for Blue Jackets' Zach Werenski
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs are interested in trading for Columbus Blue Jackets’ defenseman Zach Werenski, who had a Norris Trophy-winning campaign, according to reports. The price for such a move would be massive, say NHL analysts, and Auston Matthews' friendship with Werenski has become another talking point surrounding Toronto's pursuit of the star defenseman.

title: NFL trade rumor: Dolphins, Colts emerge as top landing spots for 49ers star
content: The San Francisco 49ers’ star player Brandon Aiyuk has found himself involved in a messy drama after he refused the team’s workouts in the last few days. While many analysts were expecting the star player will be traded to the Washington Commanders by now, nothing has been confirmed yet. Amid this, the Miami Dolphins and the Indianapolis Colts have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Maple Leafs linked to blockbuster trade for $57.9M Norris winner Zach Werenski
content: The Ottawa Senators have raised questions about goaltender Samuel Ersson following his not being offered a qualifying offer ahead of free agency. The move makes the Swedish netminder free to hit the open market on July 1, even though Ottawa just acquired him in a trade. Questions now linger about the club’s offseason strategy and plans in goal.

title: Cowboys linked to $53M Colts star in blockbuster NFL trade proposal
content: The Dallas Cowboys have been named a potential landing spot for Indianapolis Colts star Jonathan Taylor in a proposed trade. CBS Sports' Garrett Podell suggested the All-Pro running back could be available for a third-round pick if the Colts struggle in 2026. With Taylor entering the final year of his contract and coming off another standout season, Dallas could pursue him if its rushing attack fails to meet expectations.

title: Mumbai rains: 11-year-old dies as tree crushes school van carrying 15 students
content: The incident took place near Heritage Pride and was reported to the Mumbai Fire Brigade at 2.58 pm. Fire brigade personnel, police, a 108 ambulance and BMC ward staff rushed to the spot and carried out a rescue operation. Officials said all 15 students were travelling in the Universal High School van when the tree was uprooted and fell on the vehicle.

title: UP TGT result 2026 released at upessc.up.gov.in: Direct link to download scorecards here
content: The Uttar Pradesh Trained Graduate Teacher (UP TGT) result for 2026 has been officially released by the UPESSC on their website. Candidates can now check their selection status by downloading the result PDF. Those whose roll numbers are listed are considered qualified for the next recruitment phase.  It is crucial to verify all details and stay updated on the official UPESSC portal for further information.

title: Psychology says people who love jungle safaris are often high in ‘Openness to Experience’
content: Thrill-seekers drawn to jungle safaris often exhibit high 'Openness to Experience,' a personality trait linked to curiosity and a desire for novel encounters. Psychologists suggest this trait fuels the enjoyment of unpredictable wildlife sightings and the immersive journey itself. Beyond excitement, nature's embrace is found to enhance creative thinking and foster a deeper connection to the environment, inspiring conservation efforts.

title: 'I'm very happy': Jadeja reacts to Ben Stokes' shock retirement
content: Former India captain Ajay Jadeja lauded Ben Stokes' retirement, calling it the "right call" and a testament to his refusal to be dictated by external forces. Jadeja admired Stokes' principled stand, suggesting the all-rounder wouldn't tolerate administrators controlling his personal life.  Meanwhile, Sanjay Manjrekar expressed sadness, calling Stokes' departure an "end of an era" and highlighting his match-winning prowess on the big stage.

title: 40°C and counting: Europe's heatwave exposes a continent built for cold
content: Europe, once crafted to stay warm during harsh winters, now grapples with a grim new challenge: relentless heatwaves. Buildings that sheltered against frosty temperatures have turned into sweltering traps, revealing a serious flaw in adapting to intense summer heat. With infrastructure buckling under record temperatures and a surge in heat-related fatalities, a fundamental reevaluation of urban planning is desperately needed to face an increasingly hotter reality.

title: Bangladesh's Taijul Islam breaks Mitchell Starc's record, enters all-time ...
content: Bangladesh spinner Taijul Islam achieved a significant personal milestone, securing his 19th Test five-wicket haul against Zimbabwe. This feat places him among the top left-arm bowlers globally, drawing level with Shakib Al Hasan and surpassing Mitchell Starc. Despite Taijul's individual success, Zimbabwe secured their largest-ever Test victory by an innings and 85 runs.

title: Gaurav Khanna makes first public appearance after wife Akanksha's divorce announcement
content: In the wake of her separation announcement from Gaurav Khanna, Akanksha Chamola's husband made a poignant public appearance, showcasing his steadfast support. While promoting Khatron Ke Khiladi 15, Khanna articulated, "Pyaar kiya toh peeche nahi hatunga," highlighting his enduring affection for his wife. Chamola previously disclosed their mutual decision for divorce on Lock Upp 2, attributing it to their diverging life paths after spending a year apart.

title: Late Caroline Flack’s brother Paul passes away at 55
content: Paul Flack, brother of the late Caroline Flack, has passed away at 55. Found unresponsive at his Sandringham home on June 21, 2026, the cause of death remains inconclusive pending further investigation. An inquest is underway. Flack, a graphic designer and artist, was active in his local community. His sister, Caroline, tragically died by suicide in 2020.

title: Google employee shares reality behind viral office life videos, says 'Don't believe every reel'
content: A Google employee's recent post on X challenged the glamorous image of tech company life, revealing that popular amenities like gaming rooms and gyms are often underutilized.  The employee shared that while perks exist, the reality is a significant workload, suggesting social media often showcases curated moments rather than typical days.  Many users resonated with this perspective, highlighting the disconnect between online portrayals and actual corporate experiences.

title: Ram Temple trust to hold key meet 5 days early, to decide Champat Rai's fate on July 6
content: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has advanced its meeting to July 6, where the resignations of General Secretary Champat Rai and member Anil Mishra are expected to be finalized. The Trust may also discuss operational restructuring for enhanced transparency and consider new members. Discussions will also cover improving visitor facilities, especially during extreme heat, and crowd management strategies.

title: 'Hands off' Strait of Hormuz: Iran says foreign intervention to 'complicate issues'
content: Iran has confidently declared its readiness to maintain order in the Strait of Hormuz, insisting that foreign intervention is unnecessary and could destabilize the region. Furthermore, discussions with the US, arranged by Qatar, are expected to focus on the existing interim agreement rather than on new negotiations for a final settlement. Ongoing dialogue with Washington occurs through established political channels.

title: Iran-US interim peace deal may support India's growth: RBI
content: India's economy shows resilience despite global turbulence, with an interim Iran-US peace deal potentially boosting supply chains and easing geopolitical pressures, according to the RBI's Financial Stability Report. While macroeconomic fundamentals are strong, the nation remains vulnerable to imported energy price shocks. The report highlights sustained economic activity but warns of inflation risks from supply disruptions and potential fiscal deficit pressures.

title: Hormuz, Suez, Taiwan Strait: Why the world’s supply chains now need war-room planning
content: Global supply chains face unprecedented threats as maritime chokepoints become geopolitical battlegrounds. The US-Iran conflict highlighted how disruptions in vital waterways like the Strait of Hormuz and Bab el-Mandeb trigger widespread economic fallout, from soaring freight rates to increased inflation. Other critical routes, including the Suez, Taiwan, Malacca, and Panama Canals, are also under scrutiny, demanding a shift towards war-room level planning for future crises.

title: ‘I hate men’ post in Ketan death case gets dentist suspended for 5 years
content: A Madhya Pradesh dental association has suspended its treasurer, Dr. Muskan Soni, for five years after she allegedly justified the murder of a Pune businessman on social media. The association cited "acts of indiscipline" and "highly inappropriate, offensive and disrespectful remarks" as reasons for the severe action. Despite an apology, the organization deemed it insufficient, emphasizing a zero-tolerance policy for conduct undermining ethical standards and professional dignity.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 scripts unprecedented history after Germany, Netherlands crash out
content: A historic World Cup saw two top-10 nations, Germany and the Netherlands, crash out in the opening knockout round. Germany's campaign ended on penalties against Paraguay after a controversial disallowed goal. The Netherlands also fell to Morocco in a dramatic shootout, with Morocco's goalkeeper proving decisive. This marks a significant upset in the tournament's history.

title: Her son was diagnosed with autism at 2. What she did next is a lesson in parenting
content: A Mumbai mother defied dire predictions after her son Vihaan's autism diagnosis, refusing to let it define his potential.  Despite early struggles with communication and societal judgment, Sayuri Dalvi's unwavering support and focus on shared activities like running helped Vihaan thrive. Now 21, he drives, writes, and inspires thousands, proving that limitations are often societal, not inherent.

title: Ketan death case: Lohagad Fort closed for visitors as Lonavala police intensify probe
content: Lohagad Fort was temporarily closed Tuesday as police investigate the Ketan Agarwal murder. Accused Chetan Chaudhary will undergo forensic gait analysis to verify his presence at the fort on the day of the incident. Investigators also plan a fresh crime scene reconstruction with Chaudhary and hope to recover his alleged attire. Both Chaudhary and co-accused Siya Goyal are in police custody until July 3.

title: Anti-drone arsenal, radars & anti-aircraft guns: How Army is keeping Amarnath pilgrims safe
content: As the Amarnath yatra approaches, the Army has implemented a state-of-the-art security system featuring advanced drone detection technology and anti-aircraft weaponry to address aerial threats. Collaborating with various security agencies, this holistic strategy guarantees ongoing monitoring along pilgrimage routes. This comprehensive security arrangement is designed to protect the thousands of devotees participating in this important annual Hindu event.

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian equity markets dipped Tuesday, with IT stocks leading the decline. However, electric vehicle companies surged following Delhi's new EV policy, signaling a boost for clean mobility. While benchmarks faltered, mid- and small-cap segments showed resilience. Analysts suggest the market is in a consolidation phase, with positive long-term prospects for EV adoption despite broader caution.

title: Will Vaibhav Sooryavanshi finally make his India debut in Durham?
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut remains elusive despite strong IPL form, as the team suffered a shock T20I series loss to Ireland. With top-order struggles persisting, the upcoming England series presents a potential opportunity for the young batting star. Assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate confirmed Sooryavanshi's readiness but emphasised the need for patience and backing established players.

title: BSNL failed to restore landline; consumer commission orders Rs 15,000 compensation
content: A consumer's fight against BSNL has been vindicated by the Madhya Pradesh State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission. The commission upheld an order for BSNL to restore a landline connection and pay Rs 15,000 in compensation. BSNL failed to provide evidence for its claims of cable damage and inability to repair, leading the commission to dismiss their appeal as baseless.

title: 4-year-old boy falls into 220-foot-deep borewell in Haryana; rescue operation under way
content: A four-year-old boy, Nirbhay, tragically fell into a 220-foot-deep borewell in Ambala's Dhanaura village while playing near his father.  Rescue operations involving NDRF, SDRF, and the Army are underway. The incident occurred around 6:30 am when the child was reportedly throwing soil into the open borewell and slipped.  Authorities were alerted after initial rescue attempts by family and locals failed.

title: 'We didn't hold him hostage': Punjab Kings' Shashank Singh breaks silence after FIR against him, father
content: Cricketer Shashank Singh has responded to an FIR alleging assault and illegal confinement by a domestic cook. Singh claims the cook was hired under false pretenses, was taking private photos, and his mother deleted them due to privacy concerns. He denies any physical altercation or holding the cook hostage, stating they considered calling the police themselves after discovering the alleged unauthorized photography.

title: Panic in central Delhi as massive fire breaks out at Barakhamba Road restaurant
content: On Tuesday afternoon, a fire incident occurred at the Parikrama Restaurant on Barakhamba Road in central Delhi, prompting an urgent reaction from emergency crews. The Delhi Fire Service effectively controlled the fire, ensuring there were no reports of injuries. The initial outbreak took place at approximately 3:40 pm, and further details will be provided as the investigation unfolds.

title: General Dhiraj Seth Takes Charge As New Army Chief
content: The general officer succeeded General Upendra Dwivedi, who retired on Tuesday after an illustrious career spanning over 40 years in the armed forces.

title: Inter-Caste Teenage Couple Found Dead In Tamil Nadu, Police Suspect Suicide
content: According to the police, tensions had been escalating between the two families over the inter-caste relationship.

title: 5-Year-Old Girl Killed In Hit-And-Run In Surat, Horror Caught On CCTV
content: The child has been identified as Aarti Baria. Her parents work as labourers at a nearby under-construction housing complex, and she had been playing outside while they worked.

title: "PoK Not Part Of Pakistan": Protesters Warn Islamabad Of Reaching Out To India
content: During the demonstration at Eidgah Ground, speakers claimed that the region should no longer be considered under Pakistan's control and warned about seeking stronger engagement with India.

title: On Camera, School Student Stabs Teacher Over Transfer Certificate Row
content: A minor student stabbed a teacher inside a school following an argument over a Transfer Certificate (TC) in Maharashtra's Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar. The incident took place at Little Flower School in the Cantonment area of the city.

title: India Likely To Get Below-Normal Rainfall In July: Weather Office
content: Some areas of northwest and northeast India, east-central India and the eastern peninsular region are likely to receive normal to above rainfall, the IMD said.

title: International Gold Smuggling Racket Busted In Delhi, Gold Worth Rs 21 Crore Seized
content: DRI officers intercepted an international courier consignment originating from Thailand at Courier Terminal, Delhi.

title: "Want To See Where Siya Pushed Ketan": Lohagad Fort Sees 50% Rise In Tourists
content: "While approximately 1,000 tourists used to visit on holidays before, the figure has now risen to 1,500," said an official.

title: Twisha Sharma's Brother Alleges Conspiracy After Break-In At In Laws' House
content: The burglary allegedly took place on the intervening night of June 27-28, when intruders entered Giribala Singh's house and reportedly tried to steal gold jewellery and a file containing documents

title: 11-Year-Old Student Dies, 4 Injured After Tree Falls On Mumbai School Bus
content: While the injured children were rushed to a nearby hospital, rescue teams are reportedly working to free one child who remains trapped inside the vehicle.

title: Boy, 4, Falls Into 220-Foot Borewell In Ambala, Rescue Ops Underway
content: Nirvair Singh, the boy, had accompanied his father to the fields in Dhanaura village in Ambala to deliver food to his grandfather at 7 am. While playing, he slipped and fell into the open borewell.

title: Minors Detained Over Murder Made To Do Sexual Acts, Bengaluru Cop Arrested
content: A case has been registered under relevant provisions of the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (POCSO) Act, along with other applicable sections of law.

title: Wife Can't Be Denied Maintenance Because Parents Support Her: High Court
content: The family court, by its order passed in December 2023, had rejected the wife's claim for maintenance entirely while awarding Rs 3,000 per month as maintenance to each child.

title: Fire Breaks Out At Delhi's Popular Parikrama Restaurant
content: According to Delhi Fire Service, a call reporting the blaze was received at around 3:40 pm, prompting authorities to rush seven fire tenders to the spot.

title: Opinion: Blog | Pranit More To Everyone Else, How Comedy And Its Scandals Continue To Fool Us
content: The modern comedian doesn't really have an ideology. They have a target market, and jokes that cater to that target market.

title: PM Modi Chairs High-Level Meet With Top Bureaucrats
content: While the details of the discussions are not known, it was expected PM Narendra Modi would touch upon policy reforms and improve the ease of doing business

title: Centre Prepares Contingency Plan For 12 States Amid El Nino Risk
content: After days of waiting, there is finally some good news for Delhi-NCR. The southwest monsoon is likely to reach the national capital around July 3, India Meteorological Department (IMD) senior scientist Dr Naresh Kumar told NDTV in an exclusive interview on Tuesday.

title: Engineers build robotic fish that swim, dock and recharge themselves with no feeding required
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world.
